paragraph_1: Generation Fabric is a small contract-driven compiler pipeline for structured documents.
paragraph_3: `JSON Schema -> validate -> JSON content -> render -> Markdown`
paragraph_4: That lets us keep each responsibility isolated while still composing them into one deterministic workflow.
list_1: `json_schema_crud.py` is the compatibility entrypoint for the CLI.
`generation_fabric/core/` holds shared JSON and IO helpers.
`generation_fabric/json_documents/` handles generic JSON tree CRUD and schema-driven sample generation.
`generation_fabric/schema/` owns schema creation, inference, and validation.
`generation_fabric/markdown/` owns Markdown rendering, the contract registry, contract scaffolding, and Markdown import.
`generation_fabric/layout/` parses ASCII layout sketches into a governed zone taxonomy contract and audits layout coherence.
`generation_fabric/html/`, `generation_fabric/css/`, and `generation_fabric/svg/` render HTML, CSS, and SVG from the same contract using `x-html`, `x-css`, and `x-svg` annotations.
`examples/` contains canonical schema, JSON, and Markdown artifacts.
`scripts/generate_table_showcase.py` is the portable Python generator for the table showcase example.
`tests/` contains end-to-end coverage for the published behaviors.
`generation_fabric/worker_bee/` owns the migration strategy scaffold, deterministic packet planner, deterministic taxonomy scanner, and document executor.
raw_1:
If you want the full taxonomy, see [docs/module-map.md](docs/module-map.md).
If you want the implementation plan, see [docs/compiler-pipeline-roadmap.md](docs/compiler-pipeline-roadmap.md).
paragraph_5: This repository is built around a few strict rules:
list_2: file names should tell the implementation story
JSON documents provide the structured source data
Markdown is a derived artifact, not the source of truth
examples should be real, committed artifacts that prove the pipeline works
paragraph_6: That keeps the system coherent as it grows from simple file operations into a more general generation fabric.
paragraph_7: Run the CLI from the repository root:
code_3:
python json_schema_crud.py read --file user.schema.json
python json_schema_crud.py create --file user.schema.json --pointer /properties/name --value '{"type":"string"}'
python json_schema_crud.py update --file user.schema.json --pointer /properties/name/type --value string
python json_schema_crud.py delete --file user.schema.json --pointer /properties/name
paragraph_10: Validate a schema or an instance:
code_4:
python json_schema_crud.py validate --file user.schema.json
python json_schema_crud.py validate --file user.schema.json --instance '"Ada"'
python json_schema_crud.py validate --file user.schema.json --instance-file sample.json
paragraph_11: Infer a schema from sample JSON:
code_5: python json_schema_crud.py infer --sample-file sample.json --output inferred.schema.json --title Users
paragraph_12: Render Markdown from a schema contract and JSON data:
paragraph_14: Import a legacy Markdown file into a schema plus JSON contract:
paragraph_layout_ascii: Parse an ASCII layout sketch into a governed zone taxonomy contract:
code_layout_ascii: python json_schema_crud.py ascii-zones --source-file examples/value-simulator.ascii.md --page-id value-simulator --output examples/value-simulator.zones.json
paragraph_layout_html: Render semantic HTML from a zone taxonomy contract:
paragraph_layout_css: Render box-model CSS from the same zone taxonomy contract:
paragraph_layout_svg: Render an SVG drawing of the zones from their parsed bounds:
paragraph_layout_coherence: Audit the zone taxonomy and write a coherence report through the Markdown renderer:
paragraph_worker_bee: Generate a Markdown document from a worker-bee brief:
code_worker_bee: python json_schema_crud.py worker-bee-generate --brief 'Generate me a markdown file that has two ASCII sketches' --output generated/billboards.md
paragraph_worker_bee_observe: Observe a Python file and render its execution paths as Mermaid sequence diagrams:
paragraph_worker_bee_taxonomy: Scan a Python file into reusable taxonomy JSON:
paragraph_worker_bee_observe_reuse: Reuse a saved taxonomy file to render the observation Markdown:
paragraph_worker_bee_propose: Produce a provider-backed planning proposal before building the packet:
code_worker_bee_propose: python json_schema_crud.py worker-bee-propose --brief 'Create a markdown operations note for the generation fabric.' --output reports/worker-bee-proposal.json
paragraph_worker_bee_learn: Run the worker-bee learning loop to benchmark the full fabric surface:
paragraph_worker_bee_sketch: Generate an ASCII layout sketch and render every layout target from a brief:
code_worker_bee_sketch: python json_schema_crud.py worker-bee-sketch --brief 'Build an MSP consultancy page focused on delivery acceleration' --output-dir generated/sketch
list_3: `new`: create a brand-new schema file
`read`: inspect a schema or any JSON Pointer path inside it
`create`: add a new node
`update`: replace an existing node
`delete`: remove a node
`validate`: validate a schema or a subschema, plus an optional instance
`infer`: build a schema from sample JSON
`json-sample`: generate a JSON sample from a schema
`json-read`: inspect a JSON document or any JSON Pointer path inside it
`json-create`: add a new node to a JSON document
`json-update`: replace an existing node in a JSON document
`json-delete`: remove a node from a JSON document
`oneof`: attach a `oneOf` combinator
`anyof`: attach an `anyOf` combinator
`markdown`: render Markdown from a schema plus JSON data
`markdown-contract`: scaffold a canonical document contract
`markdown-import`: convert a legacy Markdown file into a schema plus JSON contract
`ascii-zones`: parse an ASCII layout sketch into a zone taxonomy contract
`layout-html`: render semantic HTML from a zone taxonomy contract
`layout-css`: render box-model CSS from a zone taxonomy contract
`layout-svg`: render an SVG drawing from a zone taxonomy contract
`layout-coherence`: audit a zone taxonomy and write a coherence report
`worker-bee-plan`: build a deterministic generation packet from a brief
`worker-bee-taxonomy`: scan a Python file into reusable taxonomy JSON
`worker-bee-observe`: observe Python execution paths and render Mermaid sequence diagrams
`worker-bee-propose`: build a provider-backed planning proposal from a brief
`worker-bee-generate`: generate Markdown, schema, and JSON artifacts from a brief
`worker-bee-sketch`: generate an ASCII sketch and render every layout target from a brief
`worker-bee-learn`: run the benchmark loop and report coverage
`interactive`: use the tiny schema shell for quick experiments
paragraph_16: The repository includes a release-notes contract that demonstrates the full pipeline:
paragraph_17: Those files serve as a golden example for how the contract, content, and rendered output should line up.
paragraph_layout_example: The repository also ships an ASCII-first layout example that proves the sketch-to-HTML pipeline:
paragraph_layout_example_2: The ASCII sketch is parsed into a governed zone taxonomy, and that single contract renders to semantic HTML, box-model CSS, and an SVG drawing, plus a deterministic coherence report. ASCII is the first authority-bearing design artifact; every other target is a derived render of the same contract.
paragraph_18: The repository uses standard-library unit tests:
list_5: ASCII layout sketch parsing and zone taxonomy
semantic HTML rendering from a zone taxonomy contract
box-model CSS and SVG rendering from the same contract
code observation and Mermaid sequence diagrams
paragraph_20: The long-term direction is to keep the pipeline layered and deterministic:
ordered_list_1: define the contract with JSON Schema
add richer document models only when editable round-tripping is required
paragraph_21: That is the generation fabric: a coherent set of modules that can scale without turning into one giant monolith.
